description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a robust, rectangular body made of dark, marbled Bakelite or plastic. The front features a prominent horizontal grille design, likely covering the speaker, and a central circular emblem or badge. The top section is slightly slanted and houses a cream-colored control panel with a large, glass-covered frequency display and several amber push buttons. On each side, there is a large, ribbed tuning or volume knob, and the back includes a visible power cord extending outward. The overall design is streamlined and characteristic of mid-20th-century radios, with a combination of smooth and ribbed surfaces and a compact, sturdy build.
description: A vintage, dark brown tabletop radio receiver with a cream control panel, ribbed grille, and large side knobs. It features a glass-covered frequency display and a classic, streamlined design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the ribbed speaker grille, central badge, and the slanted cream control panel with frequency display and buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the large tuning knob and the depth of the radio's body, with the control panel slightly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the smooth back panel, power cord, and the absence of controls or grille details.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing another large knob and the side profile of the control panel.
